teaching_arc.entry_point: FTC Part 2 (Topic 25) gave fast definite-integral evaluation; many antiderivatives need a reverse chain-rule move.
teaching_arc.progression: Translator metaphor → chain rule in reverse → substitution rule → warm-up → five-step algorithm → standard/tricky examples → constant-factor misconception → definite u-limits → optional challenge → Calc 1 finale recap.
teaching_arc.destination: Students execute u-sub reliably: choose u, find du, rewrite, integrate, back-substitute; change u-limits for definite integrals.
teaching_arc.tone: warm, visual, rigorous without intimidation, celebratory finale for Calculus 1
methods: Callback Topic 13 chain rule and Topic 25 FTC Part 2.
math_solution_steps for all worked u-sub examples.
pause_and_reveal on misconception and challenge slides.

topic_goal: Teach integration by substitution as the chain rule in reverse, closing the Calculus 1 integration arc with reliable u-sub workflow for indefinite and definite integrals.
topic_takeaways: Identify when an integral is suitable for substitution
Execute the complete process: choose u, find du, rewrite, integrate, back-substitute
Change u-limits for definite integrals without mixing methods
style_notes: Keep the tone personal, warm, and precise, as if teaching directly to a YouTube viewer.
Welcome back, do NOT re-introduce Arian from scratch.
Orient on roadmap: Topics 1–25 done, Topic 26 current, final Calculus 1 topic.
Callback Topic 25 FTC Part 2 (F(b)−F(a)) and Topic 13 chain rule when introducing reverse integration.
Use pause slides with real beats: constant-factor trap (s07), warm-up check (s04), optional challenge (s12).
math_solution_steps on s04, s06, s08, s09, s10, s12, narrate step_1 through step_N in reveal order.
s13 celebrates course completion; one-line Calc 2 preview only.
No widget on translator diagram, paragraph/nested_bullets columns suffice.
transition_style: One beat per slide: name the u-sub task, reveal steps progressively, call out traps before they stick.

slides.narration_seed: Welcome back, Topic 26 closes Calculus 1. FTC Part 2 gave F(b)−F(a); today reverse the chain rule from Topic 13.
must_cover: Final Calculus 1 topic, Integration by Substitution
Callback FTC Part 2 and chain rule (Topic 13)
must_say: This is our final topic in Calculus 1.
slides.transition_from_previous: Topic 25 ended with FTC evaluation; this topic handles integrands that need u-sub before antiderivatives are obvious.
slides.transition_to_next: Roadmap and four objectives, Topic 26 on the path.
likely_student_confusion: Thinking substitution is unrelated to the chain rule.
slides.explanation_style: Brief welcome-back, name the reverse-chain-rule hook.
slides.delivery_goal: Students know this closes the integration arc and Calc 1.

slides.narration_seed: Roadmap current path_topic_26. Four objectives: spot u-sub, five-step workflow, creative substitutions, definite u-limits.
must_cover: Topic 25 FTC completed; no next topic in Calc 1
Four skills map to today's blocks
slides.transition_from_previous: From title hook into roadmap orientation.
slides.delivery_goal: Students see where Topic 26 sits in the full course.

slides.narration_seed: Recipe translator metaphor. Three columns: foreign integral → u-language → back translation. No widget, read the column flow.
must_cover: Every x must disappear after substitution
slides.transition_from_previous: From objectives into the core metaphor.
slides.transition_to_next: Formalize with chain rule in reverse.
likely_student_confusion: Choosing u arbitrarily without matching du.
slides.delivery_goal: Students grasp u as temporary simpler language.

slides.narration_seed: Topic 13 chain rule forward; integration reverses it. formula_block left; nested_bullets on right for forward/backward mapping.
slides.transition_from_previous: From metaphor to formal reverse-chain-rule statement.
slides.transition_to_next: Substitution rule with u and du.
slides.delivery_goal: Students connect u-sub to chain rule.

slides.narration_seed: Indefinite integral substitution formula. Choose u, get du, replace all x. Wrong u leaves x behind.
slides.transition_from_previous: From reverse chain rule to formal substitution rule.
likely_student_confusion: Leaving x in the integral after substituting.
slides.delivery_goal: Students state the substitution rule correctly.

slides.narration_seed: math_solution_steps: step_1 u=x³+1, step_2 du=3x²dx, step_3 ∫u⁴du, step_4 power rule, step_5 back-sub. Pause: differentiate answer to verify.
slides.transition_from_previous: First worked example after the rule.
likely_student_confusion: Use the prompt: What is the derivative of (x³+1)⁵/5?
slides.delivery_goal: Students follow choose u → du → integrate → back-sub.

slides.narration_seed: nested_bullets five-step checklist. Guardrails: no x left, constant factor trap preview.
must_cover: Five steps: choose u, find du, rewrite, integrate, back-substitute
If x remains after rewrite, different u

slides.narration_seed: ∫√(2x+1) dx: step_1 u=2x+1, step_2 dx=du/2, step_3 ½∫u^{1/2}du, step_4 power rule, step_5 back-sub. Alternative u=√(2x+1) mentioned.
likely_student_confusion: Use the prompt: Try setting u = √(2x+1) and verify.
slides.delivery_goal: Students handle dx = du/k after substitution.

slide_ref.source_title: Common Mistake: Forgetting the Constant Factor (Pause)

slides.narration_seed: misconception_compare: wrong ∫cos u vs correct ½∫cos u. Pause reveals ½sin(x²)+C. ∫x cos(x²) dx.
must_cover: du = 2x dx but integrand has x dx
likely_student_confusion: Use the prompt: What would the correct final answer be?
slides.delivery_goal: Students always solve for matching dx piece.

slides.narration_seed: ∫x/√(1−4x²) dx: step_1 u=1−4x², step_2 du=−8x dx, step_3 −⅛∫u^{-1/2}du, step_4 integrate, step_5 back-sub with minus sign.
slides.transition_from_previous: After constant-factor trap, add sign trap.
likely_student_confusion: Use the prompt: What if you forgot the negative sign?
slides.story_hint: Signs matter as much as constants.

slides.narration_seed: ∫(ln x)/x dx: u=ln x, du=(1/x)dx. Function-derivative pair, not nested composite. Contrast ∫e^x/x dx, no elementary u-sub.
must_cover: ln x and 1/x as function-derivative pair
∫e^x/x dx does not yield to elementary substitution
likely_student_confusion: Use the prompt: Does substitution work on ∫e^x/x dx?

slides.narration_seed: Definite rule + math_solution_steps for ∫₀¹4x e^{x²}dx. step_3 transform limits x→u. step_5 answer 2(e−1). Never mix methods.
must_cover: Worked example with u-limits step_1 through step_5
slides.transition_from_previous: Callback Topic 25 FTC, now with u-sub inside.
slides.delivery_goal: Students change u-limits correctly for definite integrals.

slides.narration_seed: Same integral as s10, riemann_integral shades area under 4x e^{x²} on [0,1]. Area ≈ 2(e−1). Connect u-sub to net area.
slides.explanation_style: Widget narration, increase n on timeline.
slides.delivery_goal: Students connect u-sub answer to shaded area.

slides.narration_seed: Pause first. ∫e^{√x}/√x dx: u=√x, x=u², dx=2u du. math_solution_steps step_1–step_3. Optional, skippable.
must_cover: u = √x requires solving for x and dx
u cancels in numerator and denominator
likely_student_confusion: Use the prompt: Try it yourself before revealing the steps.
slides.story_hint: Nested u, solve for x completely.
slides.delivery_goal: Optional stretch, students see dx from u-sub fully.

slides.narration_seed: Full integration arc Topics 22–26. Celebrate 26 topics complete. Substitution recap bullets. One-line Calc 2: parts and partial fractions.
must_cover: Integration arc: antiderivatives → Riemann → definite → FTC → substitution
slides.transition_from_previous: Consolidate entire S05 arc and full course.
slides.story_hint: Finale, thank the audience for the journey.
slides.delivery_goal: Students leave with integration arc memory and course pride.
